behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file
behaviors.justification: To verify the agent respects diff boundaries, you need to coordinate diff parsing with analysis or input preparation. A Unit test of input extraction alone could pass while the analysis step silently includes full-file content. Mock-miss scenario: extract the right subset but the LLM is called with full file anyway. Integration level catches the coordination.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a PR diff with changes on specific lines (e.g., lines 10-20 in a 100-line file). Mock the PR API, run the agent's analysis step, and verify that analysis output only references the changed lines—not any unchanged lines from the file. Verify against a ground-truth list of changed line ranges.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request
behaviors.justification: GitHub (and similar platforms) use position-in-diff coordinates, not line numbers. A mock test verifying the API call contains correct parameters could hide bugs in position calculation. The phrase 'in the diff view on the pull request' requires actual verification on the PR. Only System-level testing (real or simulated PR) will catch misaligned position references.
behaviors.test_description: Post inline comments to a real PR (or realistic PR simulator) with known changes. Navigate to the PR diff view and verify each comment appears at the exact line where it was intended. Check that position-in-diff coordinates match the visual placement on GitHub.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends entirely on LLM output, which is non-deterministic. Mocking the LLM to return 'defect found' always passes the test but hides real failures (real LLM misses the bug). Running the same test 10 times with the real LLM will give different results. This is the definition of Agentic-level dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create a PR with a known introduced bug (e.g., null pointer dereference, off-by-one logic error, missing error handling). Run the agent with a real LLM against this PR. Verify at least one of the findings correctly identifies the known bug, or captures its symptoms. Optionally, run the test multiple times to measure detection rate.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted—no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment
behaviors.justification: This is a consistency check between two subsystems: inline comment generation and summary generation. You can mock the PR API and capture all calls, then verify the correlation logic. The failure would be caught if you collect all inline comment identifiers (file:line:text) and all findings in the summary, then assert a bijection. No LLM non-determinism; no external API verification needed.
behaviors.test_description: Run the agent with a mocked PR API. Collect all inline comment posts (capture file, line, and comment text). Collect all findings listed in the top-level summary. Verify every finding in the summary has a corresponding inline comment with matching content. Verify no inline comments are orphaned (comment posted but not mentioned in summary).

behaviors.description: The full sequence—reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment—completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention
behaviors.justification: The agent orchestrates multiple steps (read diff → analyze → post comments → post summary), likely involving multiple LLM calls (problem identification, summary generation). The workflow must complete without requiring user input (e.g., retry prompts, missing credentials). Failures in one step (e.g., error after posting comments but before summary) need recovery logic. This is multi-step agentic orchestration, not a single LLM call.
behaviors.test_description: Set up a real or realistic test PR with changes. Run the agent end-to-end without any manual intervention. Verify: (1) no exceptions or non-zero exit codes, (2) all inline comments are posted to the PR, (3) the top-level summary comment is posted to the PR, (4) the agent completes within a reasonable time. If the test fails partway, verify the agent either recovers or exits cleanly with clear error messages.
